v2_compliance.inline_visual: pad-note (Iowa SF 607 callout in SUI wage-base section)

factual_uncertainty_resolved.source: Iowa Workforce Development press release 5 September 2025; Iowa SF 607 (signed 5 June 2025) reduced wage-base formula from two-thirds to one-third of prior-year statewide average annual wage

factual_uncertainty_resolved.spec_claim: Iowa hit flat 3.8% in 2026 ahead of original 2026 schedule
factual_uncertainty_resolved.verified_actual: Flat 3.8% took effect in 2025, one year ahead of original Iowa HF 2317 (2022) schedule which set it for 2026. Acceleration via Iowa SF 2442 (May 2024). 2026 is the second flat-tax year, not the first.
factual_uncertainty_resolved.source: Iowa DOR press release 21 October 2025; Iowa SF 2442 (May 2024)

factual_uncertainty_resolved.verified_actual: 1.000 percent for non-construction; 5.400 percent for construction industry
factual_uncertainty_resolved.source: Iowa Workforce Development UI Employer Handbook (Table D 2026)

flags.detail: Spec said $39,500 UI wage base for 2026. Actual is $20,400 (spec referenced 2025 figure). Corrected in page + compliance cache.
Spec said Iowa hit flat 3.8% in 2026. Actual: 2025 was the first flat-tax year (accelerated via Iowa SF 2442 May 2024); 2026 is the second. Corrected in page + cache.
